pr.title: Fix lbsnap split, move some of the bss into files, split sbss out of bss.s
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: PR 132 was a low-level assembly/data-layout cleanup. It fixed the lbsnap split by moving func_8001D2BC from asm/melee/lb/code_80016A54.s into asm/melee/lb/lbsnap.s and adjusted lbsnap's text range comment from 0x8001D338 to 0x8001D2BC. It also reduced the monolithic asm/bss.s by moving several early .bss labels into their owning asm files and extracted the entire .sbss block into a new asm/sbss.s file wired through obj_files.mk.
what_changed: Deleted 3657 lines from asm/bss.s, including a large inline .sbss section and several early .bss label reservations.
Added new asm/sbss.s containing a standalone .section .sbss range comment for 0x804D63A0 - 0x804D79C9 and the former lbl_804D63A0 through lbl_804D79C8-style .skip reservations.
Updated obj_files.mk so SBSS_O_FILES is no longer empty and now includes $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sbss.o.
Moved func_8001D2BC out of asm/melee/lb/code_80016A54.s and into asm/melee/lb/lbsnap.s, making lbsnap.s start at 0x8001D2BC instead of 0x8001D338.

Moved .bss labels from the central bss.s into module asm files: lbl_804316C0/lbl_804318B0/lbl_80431FA0 into code_8000E9F0.s, lbl_80432058 into lbfile.s, lbl_80432078 through lbl_80433380 into code_80016A54.s, lbl_804333E0 through lbl_80433B44 into code_8001E2F8.s, and lbl_80452C68/lbl_80453004/lbl_80453060 into code_80028B9C.s.
smart_moves: Separated .sbss from asm/bss.s instead of leaving two sections mixed inside one monolithic bss file; this made the section boundary explicit and required a corresponding SBSS_O_FILES build entry.
Preserved placeholder symbol names and exact .skip sizes while relocating data, minimizing risk to matching and link layout.
Corrected the lbsnap file boundary by moving the whole function at 0x8001D2BC with its labels and branch targets, rather than only changing comments or object ordering.
Moved chunks of .bss into nearby domain files, which is a useful intermediate step before those asm files become C and can own their data.
decomp_lessons: When splitting asm files, check both function boundaries and data ownership; a misplaced function such as func_8001D2BC can make a named split misleading even if the bytes still assemble.
Section extraction must be paired with build-system object-list changes. Creating asm/sbss.s would be incomplete without adding asm/sbss.o to SBSS_O_FILES.
For early decomp data movement, preserving address-derived lbl_XXXXXXXX names and exact .skip sizes is safer than inventing semantic variable names without stronger evidence.
Large monolithic bss/sbss files can be chipped away by moving contiguous address ranges into the asm file that references or logically owns them, while keeping unresolved labels address-based.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data symbols kept the lbl_XXXXXXXX address-label convention, e.g. lbl_804316C0, lbl_80433380, lbl_804D63A0.
Unidentified functions kept the func_XXXXXXXX convention, e.g. func_8001D2BC.
Assembly split files used address-based names where no semantic name was available, e.g. code_80016A54.s and code_8001E2F8.s.
Build variables followed section/object naming conventions: BSS_O_FILES, SBSS_O_FILES, SDATA_O_FILES, SDATA2_O_FILES.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used .section .bss, "wa" for relocated BSS reservations in module asm files.
Created a dedicated .section .sbss file with an address-range comment matching the moved region.
Preserved .global declarations and .skip reservation sizes exactly across moves.
Adjusted the lbsnap .text range comment to reflect the new first function address after moving func_8001D2BC.
Kept relocation mechanical: remove from asm/bss.s, add equivalent labels/skips to the target asm files, and include the new object in obj_files.mk.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace lbl_80433380 with a typed structure or named variable.
Check whether asm/sbss.s was later split further into per-module C or asm ownership.
Search for subsequent lbsnap decompilation work around func_8001D2BC and CARDProbe.
Look for later object-order changes involving SBSS_O_FILES or bss/sbss section layout.
